def_before: the use of something in a way that is wrong or harmful|unfair, cruel or violent treatment of somebody|rude and offensive remarks, usually made when somebody is very angry|to make bad use of something, or to use so much of something that it harms your health|to use power or knowledge unfairly or wrongly|to treat a person or an animal in a cruel or violent way, especially sexually|to make rude or offensive remarks to or about somebody
gloss_after: (noun) misuse of something | cruel treatment | offensive remarks  |  (verb) misuse or overconsume | exploit or mistreat | speak offensively

def_before: able to think quickly; quick to notice things|aware of something, especially a problem or danger|a situation in which people are watching for danger and ready to deal with it|a warning of danger or of a problem|a signal on an electronic device to inform the user of something or remind them to do something|to warn somebody about a dangerous situation or one that requires immediate action|to make somebody aware of something
gloss_after: (adj) mentally quick | aware of danger  |  (noun) state of watchfulness | warning of danger | electronic notification  |  (verb) warn of danger | make aware

def_before: doing something for pleasure or interest, not as a job|done for pleasure, not as a job|not done or made well or with skill|a person who takes part in a sport or other activity for pleasure, not as a job|a person who does not have enough skill to be able to do something well
gloss_after: (adj) non-professional | done for pleasure | unprofessional or unskilled  |  (noun) non-professional participant | unskilled person

def_before: the crime of attacking somebody physically|the act of attacking a building, an area, etc. in order to take control of it|the act of trying to achieve something that is difficult or dangerous|an act of criticizing somebody/something severely|to attack somebody violently, especially when this is a crime
gloss_after: (noun) physical attack | military onslaught | difficult attempt  |  (verb) attack violently

def_before: a quality or feature of somebody/something|to say or believe that something is the result of a particular thing|to say or believe that somebody is responsible for doing something, especially for saying, writing or painting something|to regard a quality or feature as belonging to somebody/something
gloss_after: (noun) quality or feature  |  (verb) credit to source | assign quality

def_before: an offer by a person or a company to pay a particular amount of money for something|an offer to do work or provide a service for a particular price, in competition with other companies, etc.|to offer to pay a particular price for something, especially at an auction|to offer to do work or provide a service for a particular price, in competition with other companies, etc.
gloss_after: (noun) price offer | contract proposal  |  (verb) offer price | submit proposal

def_before: an explosion or a powerful movement of air caused by an explosion|a sudden strong movement of air|a sudden loud noise, especially one made by a musical instrument that you blow, or by a whistle or a car horn|to violently destroy or break something into pieces, using explosives|to make a loud unpleasant noise, especially music
gloss_after: (noun) explosion | gust of wind | sudden loud noise  |  (verb) blow up | play loudly

def_before: a mixture of different types of the same thing|a pleasant or useful combination of different things|to mix two or more substances together|to form a mixture with something|to combine with something in an attractive or effective way; to combine something in this way|to produce something by mixing different types together
gloss_after: (noun) mixture | harmonious combination  |  (verb) mix together | combine harmoniously

def_before: the act of bending your head or the upper part of your body forward in order to say hello or goodbye to somebody or to show respect|a weapon used for shooting arrows, consisting of a long curved piece of wood or metal with a tight string joining its ends|a knot with two loops and two loose ends which is used for decoration on clothes, in hair, etc. or for tying shoes|a long thin piece of wood with thin string stretched along it, used for playing musical instruments such as the violin|to move your head or the top half of your body forwards and downwards as a sign of respect or to say hello or goodbye|to move your head forwards and downwards
gloss_after: (noun) respectful bend | archer weapon | looped knot  |  (verb) bend body forward | lower head

def_before: a failure to do something that must be done by law|an action that breaks an agreement to behave in a particular way|a break in a relationship between people or countries|to not keep to an agreement or not keep a promise
gloss_after: (noun) violation of law | broken agreement | rift in relationship  |  (verb) violate or break

def_before: an agreement made between two people or groups in which each side gives up some of the things they want so that both sides are happy at the end|a solution to a problem in which two or more things cannot exist together as they are, in which each thing is reduced or changed slightly so that they can exist together|the act of reaching a compromise|to give up some of your demands in order to reach an agreement after disagreeing with somebody|to do something that is against your principles or does not reach standards that you have set|to cause somebody/something/yourself to be in danger or to be suspected of something, especially by acting in a way that is not very sensible
gloss_after: (noun) mutual concession agreement | middle-ground solution  |  (verb) concede demands | betray principles | put in danger

def_before: a line on the surface of something where it has broken but not split into separate parts|a narrow space or opening|to break without dividing into separate parts; to break something in this way|to break open or into pieces; to break something in this way
gloss_after: (noun) hairline fracture | narrow opening  |  (verb) fracture slightly | break open

def_before: very popular with a particular group of people|a way of life, an attitude, an idea, etc. that has become very popular|a small group of people who have extreme religious beliefs and who are not part of any established religion|a system of religious beliefs and practices
gloss_after: (adj) highly popular locally  |  (noun) popular trend | religious sect | belief system

def_before: a feeling of great worry or unhappiness; great mental pain|severe problems caused by not having enough money, food, etc.|a situation in which a ship, plane, etc. is in danger or difficulty and needs help|to make somebody feel very worried or unhappy
gloss_after: (noun) mental suffering | severe hardship | danger or trouble  |  (verb) make worried or unhappy

def_before: a jump into deep water with your head first and your arms in front of you|an act of going underwater and swimming there with special equipment|an act of suddenly flying downwards|to jump into water with your head and arms going in first|to swim underwater wearing breathing equipment, collecting or looking at things|to go to a deeper level underwater|to go steeply down through the air|to move or jump quickly in a particular direction, especially to avoid something, to try to catch a ball, etc.

def_before: the reflecting of sound off a wall or inside a particular space so that a noise appears to be repeated; a sound that is reflected back in this way|if a sound echoes, it is reflected off a wall, the side of a mountain, etc. so that you can hear it again|to send back and repeat a sound; to be full of a sound|to repeat an idea or opinion because you agree with it
gloss_after: (noun) repeated sound reflection  |  (verb) reflect sound | repeat sound | mimic idea

def_before: fairly hard; not easy to press into a different shape|not likely to change|strongly fixed in place|strong and steady|strong and in control|a business or company
gloss_after: (adj) solid or hard | unlikely to change | strongly fixed  |  (noun) business or company

def_before: a strong hold of somebody/something or control over somebody/something|a person’s understanding of a subject or of difficult facts|to take a strong hold of somebody/something|to understand something completely
gloss_after: (noun) tight hold | complete understanding  |  (verb) hold tightly | understand completely

def_before: an act of holding somebody/something tightly; a particular way of doing this|control or power over somebody/something|an understanding of something|the ability of something to move over a surface without slipping|to hold something tightly|to interest or have a strong effect on somebody; to hold somebody’s attention|to have a powerful effect on somebody/something
gloss_after: (noun) tight hold | control or power | complete understanding  |  (verb) hold tightly | hold attention | affect powerfully

def_before: something that you say or do in an indirect way in order to show somebody what you are thinking|something that suggests what will happen in the future|a small amount of something|a small piece of practical information or advice|to suggest something in an indirect way
gloss_after: (noun) indirect suggestion | sign of future | small amount  |  (verb) suggest indirectly

def_before: a small hole that lets liquid or gas flow in or out of something by accident|liquid or gas that escapes through a hole in something|a deliberate act of giving secret information to reporters, etc.|to allow liquid or gas to get in or out through a small hole|to get in or out through a small hole in something|to give secret information to the public, for example by telling a reporter
gloss_after: (noun) accidental hole | escaped substance | disclosure of secrets  |  (verb) escape through hole | disclose secret information

def_before: a long or high jump|a sudden large increase in something|a sudden change from one thing to another; very fast progress with something|to jump high or a long way|to move or do something suddenly and quickly|to increase suddenly and by a large amount
gloss_after: (noun) high jump | sudden large increase | rapid transition  |  (verb) jump high | move quickly | increase suddenly

def_before: willing to understand and respect other people’s behaviour, opinions, etc., especially when they are different from your own; believing people should be able to choose how they behave|following or supporting political policies that encourage social change and support the sharing of wealth more equally|having or relating to political and social beliefs that support individual freedom and rights, democracy and free enterprise (= businesses competing against each other with little government control)|a person who understands and respects other people’s opinions and behaviour, especially when they are different from their own|a person who supports political, social or religious change and the more equal sharing of wealth
gloss_after: (adj) open-minded | supporting social change | supporting individual freedom  |  (noun) open-minded person | supporter of change

def_before: a thick piece of wood that is cut from or has fallen from a tree|an official record of events during a particular period of time, especially a journey on a ship or plane|a written or digital record of activity on a computer or phone line|to put information in an official record or write a record of events
gloss_after: (noun) piece of wood | official event record | digital activity record  |  (verb) record officially

def_before: an organized walk by many people from one place to another, in order to protest about something, or to express their opinions|an act of marching; a journey made by marching|to walk with stiff regular steps like a soldier|to walk somewhere quickly in a determined way|to walk through the streets in a large group in order to protest about something|to force somebody to walk somewhere with you
gloss_after: (noun) protest walk | soldierly walk  |  (verb) walk like soldier | walk determinedly | walk in protest

def_before: a friend|used as a friendly way of addressing somebody, especially between men|a person you share an activity or accommodation with|the sexual partner of a bird or animal|to have sex in order to produce young
gloss_after: (noun) close friend | activity partner | animal sexual partner  |  (verb) breed or copulate

def_before: behaving in a sensible way, like an adult|fully grown and developed|to become fully grown or developed|to develop emotionally and start to behave like a sensible adult
gloss_after: (adj) sensible or adult | fully developed  |  (verb) grow or develop | develop emotional maturity

def_before: two or more lines that are parallel to each other are the same distance apart at every point|very similar or taking place at the same time|a person, a situation, an event, etc. that is very similar to another, especially one in a different place or time|similar features
gloss_after: (adj) equidistant | very similar  |  (noun) similar situation | similar features

def_before: a number of things that have been placed on top of each other|a mass of something that is high in the middle and wider at the bottom than at the top|a lot of something|to put things one on top of another; to form a pile|to put something on/into something; to load something with something
gloss_after: (noun) stacked things | heap or mound | large amount  |  (verb) stack up | load or gather

def_before: a small plastic object with two or three metal pins that connects a piece of electrical equipment to the main supply of electricity|a small opening in a wall, by which you connect a piece of electrical equipment to the main supply of electricity|a small object that connects a wire from one piece of electrical equipment to an opening in another|a thick round piece of plastic, rubber or metal that you put in the hole in a bath or a sink to stop the water flowing out|to fill a hole with a substance or piece of material that fits tightly into it|to provide something that has been missing from a particular situation and is needed in order to improve it
gloss_after: (noun) electrical connector | socket or outlet | hole stopper  |  (verb) fill a hole | supply missing element

def_before: a short surprise attack on an enemy by soldiers, ships or aircraft|a surprise visit by the police looking for criminals or for illegal goods or drugs|an attack on a building, etc. in order to commit a crime|to visit a person or place without warning to look for criminals, illegal goods, drugs, etc.|to attack a place without warning|to enter a place, usually using force, and steal from it
gloss_after: (noun) surprise military attack | police surprise search | criminal break-in  |  (verb) search without warning | attack unexpectedly | break in and steal

def_before: a large public meeting, especially one held to support a particular idea or political party|a race for cars, motorcycles, etc. over public roads|to come together or bring people together in order to help or support somebody/something|to increase in value after falling in value
gloss_after: (noun) public support meeting | vehicular race  |  (verb) gather for support | increase in value

def_before: a person who has recently joined the armed forces or the police|a person who joins an organization, a company, etc.|to find new people to join a company, an organization, the armed forces, etc.|to form a new army, team, etc. by persuading new people to join it
gloss_after: (noun) new military member | new organization member  |  (verb) enlist new people | form by enlisting

def_before: a movement away from a place or an enemy because of danger or defeat|an act of trying to escape from a particular situation to one that you think is safer or more pleasant|to move away from a place or an enemy because you are in danger or because you have been defeated|to move away or back|to escape to a place that is quieter or safer
gloss_after: (noun) movement away | escape from situation  |  (verb) move away from danger | move back | escape to safety

def_before: opposite to what has been mentioned|the opposite of what has just been mentioned|the machine in a vehicle used to make it move backwards|to change something completely so that it is the opposite of what it was before|to change a previous decision, law, etc. to the opposite one|to turn something the opposite way around or change the order of something around|to exchange the positions or functions of two things|to admit you were wrong or to stop having a particular position in an argument|when a vehicle or its driver reverses or the driver reverses a vehicle, the vehicle goes backwards
gloss_after: (adj) opposite or contrary  |  (noun) opposite thing | backward vehicle gear  |  (verb) change to opposite | overturn decision | drive backwards

def_before: the state or process of being destroyed or severely damaged|the parts of a building that remain after it has been destroyed or severely damaged|the fact of having no money, of having lost your job, position, etc.|to damage something so badly that it loses all its value, pleasure, etc.|to make somebody/something lose all their money, their position, etc.
gloss_after: (noun) state of destruction | building remains | state of bankruptcy  |  (verb) damage severely | bankrupt somebody

def_before: the fact of giving up something important or valuable to you in order to get or do something that seems more important; something that you give up in this way|the act of offering something to a god, especially an animal that has been killed in a special way; an animal, etc. that is offered in this way|to give up something that is important or valuable to you in order to get or do something that seems more important for yourself or for another person|to kill an animal or a person and offer it or them to a god, in order to please the god
gloss_after: (noun) giving up something | religious offering  |  (verb) give up for others | offer to god

def_before: a situation in which a lot of people are anxious or frightened about something|a sudden feeling of fear|to frighten somebody|to become frightened
gloss_after: (noun) widespread public panic | sudden feeling of fear  |  (verb) frighten someone | become frightened

def_before: a mark, a cut or an injury made by scratching somebody’s skin or the surface of something|to rub your skin with your nails, usually because it is itching|to cut or damage your skin slightly with something sharp|to damage the surface of something, especially by accident, by making thin shallow marks on it
gloss_after: (noun) surface mark or cut  |  (verb) rub itchy skin | cut slightly | damage surface

def_before: a thin pointed piece of metal like a nail with a raised spiral line (called a thread) along it and a line or cross cut into its head. Screws are turned and pressed into wood, metal, etc. with a screwdriver in order to fasten two things together.|to fasten one thing to another or make something tight with a screw or screws|to turn something round and round in order to fasten it in place|to be attached by screwing
gloss_after: (noun) threaded metal fastener  |  (verb) fasten with metal thread | turn to tighten

def_before: to close an envelope, etc. by sticking the edges of the opening together|to close a container tightly or fill a small opening, etc., especially so that air, liquid, etc. cannot get in or out|to make something definite, so that it cannot be changed or argued about|an official design or mark, stamped on a document to show that it is real and carries the authority of a particular person or organization|a thing that makes something definite|a substance, piece of material, etc. used to fill a small opening so that air, liquid, etc. cannot get in or out|a piece of wax (= a soft substance produced by bees), soft metal or paper that is placed across the opening of something such as a letter or box and which has to be broken before the letter or box can be opened
gloss_after: (verb) close tightly | make agreement final  |  (noun) official stamp | tight closure | wax lock

def_before: the length of time that something lasts or is able to continue|to last all through a period of time or to cover the whole of it|to include a large area or a lot of things|to stretch right across something, from one side to the other

def_before: a very fast turning movement|the way you make a ball turn very fast when you throw it or hit it|to turn round and round quickly; to make something do this|to turn round quickly once; to make somebody do this

def_before: a sudden increase of a strong feeling|a sudden increase in the amount or number of something|a sudden, strong movement forward or upwards|a sudden increase in the flow of electrical power through a system|to move quickly and with force in a particular direction|to fill somebody with a strong feeling|to suddenly increase in value|to increase suddenly
gloss_after: (noun) sudden emotional increase | sudden quantity increase | sudden forward movement  |  (verb) move with force | fill with emotion | increase suddenly

def_before: the act of causing somebody severe pain in order to punish them or make them say or do something|mental or physical pain; something that causes this|to hurt somebody physically or mentally in order to punish them or make them tell you something|to make somebody feel extremely unhappy or anxious
gloss_after: (noun) severe pain punishment | great mental distress  |  (verb) inflict severe pain | cause deep anxiety

def_before: a long line or series of marks that is left by somebody/something|a track, sign or smell that is left behind and that can be followed, especially in hunting|a path through the countryside|a route that is followed for a particular purpose|to pull something behind somebody/something, usually along the ground; to be pulled along in this way|to walk slowly because you are tired or bored, especially behind somebody else; to go from one place to another without enthusiasm|to be losing a game or other contest
gloss_after: (noun) series of marks | track or scent | countryside path  |  (verb) drag behind | walk slowly | lose in contest

def_before: a piece of equipment for catching animals|a clever plan designed to trick somebody, either by capturing them or by making them do or say something that they did not mean to do or say|to put somebody in a dangerous place that they cannot get out of|to keep somebody in a bad situation that they want to get out of but cannot|to have part of your body, your clothing, etc. held in a place so tightly that you cannot remove it and it may be injured or damaged|to force somebody/something into a place or situation that they cannot escape from, especially in order to catch them
gloss_after: (noun) animal catching device | deceptive trick  |  (verb) capture in place | keep in bad situation | hold part tightly

def_before: the action of turning something with your hand, or of turning a part of your body|an unexpected change or development in a story or situation|a sharp bend in a road or river|to bend or turn something into a particular shape|to bend or turn something into a shape or position that is not normal or natural; to be bent or turned in this way|to turn part of your body around while the rest stays still|to turn your body with quick, sharp movements and change direction often|to turn something around in a circle with your hand|to bend and change direction often|to injure part of your body, especially your ankle, wrist or knee, bending it in an uncomfortable way|to wind something around or through an object|to move or grow by winding around something
gloss_after: (noun) turning movement | surprise plot turn | sharp bend  |  (verb) bend or turn | rotate | wind around
